summary: Completed testing of Feature #3: Deterministic document classification with manual override. All 21 backend tests passed. All 12 frontend UI tests passed. classify_document() correctly classifies sides, instructions, and wardrobe content. Mixed/uncertain docs default to 'unknown'. Upload endpoint auto-classifies when doc_type='unknown' and stores suggested_type field. Manual type override via PUT /api/documents/{id}/type works correctly. Frontend shows 'auto-detected' label when type matches suggested_type (except for 'unknown'). 1-tap type badge override changes type immediately.
passed_tests: classify_document() - sides with INT./EXT. scene headings returns 'sides' - PASS
classify_document() - sides with ALL CAPS dialogue pattern returns 'sides' - PASS
classify_document() - sides with EPISODE/SCENE/ACT markers returns 'sides' - PASS
classify_document() - instructions with self-tape/deadline/slate keywords returns 'instructions' - PASS
classify_document() - instructions with audition/callback/casting keywords returns 'instructions' - PASS
classify_document() - wardrobe with costume/wear/logos keywords returns 'wardrobe' - PASS
classify_document() - wardrobe with clothing/accessories keywords returns 'wardrobe' - PASS
classify_document() - mixed signals handled correctly (returns 'unknown' or dominant type) - PASS
classify_document() - short text returns 'unknown' - PASS
classify_document() - empty text returns 'unknown' - PASS
classify_document() - generic text returns 'unknown' - PASS
classify_document() - weak signals (score < 3) returns 'unknown' - PASS
Upload with doc_type='unknown' auto-classifies sides content - PASS
Upload with doc_type='unknown' auto-classifies instructions content - PASS
Upload with doc_type='unknown' auto-classifies wardrobe content - PASS
Upload stores suggested_type field alongside type - PASS
Upload with explicit type skips auto-classify - PASS
PUT /api/documents/{id}/type - manual override changes type - PASS
PUT /api/documents/{id}/type - manual override preserves suggested_type - PASS
PUT /api/documents/{id}/type - all valid types work - PASS
PUT /api/documents/{id}/type - invalid type returns 400 - PASS
Frontend: Paste type dropdown defaults to 'unknown' - PASS
Frontend: Sides content auto-classified and shows 'auto-detected' label - PASS
Frontend: Instructions content auto-classified and shows 'auto-detected' label - PASS
Frontend: Wardrobe content auto-classified and shows 'auto-detected' label - PASS
Frontend: 'unknown' type does NOT show 'auto-detected' label - PASS
Frontend: 1-tap type badge override changes type immediately - PASS
Frontend: Manual override removes 'auto-detected' label (type !== suggested_type) - PASS
Frontend: Document cards show correct type badges - PASS
Frontend: Mobile viewport (390x844) layout correct - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: classify_document() function (server.py lines 1039-1105) uses scoring system with clear thresholds
Sides signals: INT./EXT. headings (+3), ALL CAPS names (+1), EPISODE/SCENE/ACT markers (+2), dialogue pattern (+1)
Instructions signals: self-tape, deadline, slate, callback, audition, casting keywords (+2 each occurrence)
Wardrobe signals: wardrobe, costume, wear, logos, clothing keywords (+3 each occurrence)
Mixed signal detection: if top two scores ratio > 0.5, returns 'unknown'
Upload endpoint (line 1243) calls classify_document() when doc_type=='unknown'
Frontend DocumentUpload.jsx correctly shows 'auto-detected' when type===suggested_type && suggested_type!=='unknown' (line 300-302)
seed_data_creation: Test projects created with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Document classification (Feature #3) fully tested. Key function: classify_document() in server.py. Scoring: sides (scene headings, dialogue), instructions (self-tape, deadline, slate), wardrobe (costume, wear, logos). Mixed signals or weak scores return 'unknown'. Upload auto-classifies when doc_type='unknown'. Frontend shows 'auto-detected' label when type===suggested_type and suggested_type!=='unknown'. 1-tap type badges allow manual override. Data-testid: paste-type-select (defaults to 'unknown'), doc-type-{id}-{type} for type badges.

classify_sides.details: INT./EXT. headings, ALL CAPS names, EPISODE/SCENE/ACT markers correctly classified as 'sides'

classify_instructions.details: self-tape, deadline, slate, callback, audition, casting keywords correctly classified as 'instructions'

classify_wardrobe.details: wardrobe, costume, wear, logos, clothing keywords correctly classified as 'wardrobe'

suggested_type_field.details: suggested_type field stored alongside type on upload

paste_type_default.details: Paste type dropdown defaults to 'unknown' (triggers auto-classify)

one_tap_type_override.details: Clicking type badge changes type immediately
